# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf
# Category: command_injection

namespace :backup do
  desc "Create a tar.gz backup. Usage: rake backup:archive[name,path]"
  task :archive, [:name, :path] => :environment do |_, args|
    name = args[:name].to_s
    path = args[:path].to_s

    if name.empty? || path.empty?
      abort "Usage: rake backup:archive[name,path]"
    end

    timestamp = Time.now.strftime("%Y%m%d%H%M%S")
    archive = "#{name}-#{timestamp}.tar.gz"

    cmd = "tar -czf #{archive} #{path}"
    puts "Running: #{cmd}"
    output = %x{#{cmd}}
    puts output unless output.to_s.empty?

    unless $?.success?
      abort "tar failed with status #{$?.exitstatus}"
    end

    puts "Created #{archive}"
  end
end
